mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is pinned near the zero lower bound, the persistent gap between risk-free rates and equity earnings yield mechanically compresses the equity risk premium, driving continued upward drift in S&P 500 forward 20-day returns as investors rotate from cash into equities.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns during near-zero FEDFUNDS periods are not significantly higher than the full-sample mean (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month spike in UNRATE above 8% signals acute labor market deterioration that triggers immediate risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following 5 trading days as markets price in weaker consumer demand.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE > 8.0%, or Rank IC is not statistically distinguishable from zero (p > 0.10) across the sample.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in PAYEMS and INDPRO indicates a synchronized production and employment recovery that compounds into a durable earnings upswing, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days beyond what either series predicts alone.
failure_condition: The joint-signal Rank IC does not exceed the maximum of the two single-series Rank ICs by at least 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, indicating no true interaction effect.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in RSAFS reflects strengthening consumer spending momentum that feeds directly into corporate revenue expectations, producing a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward 20-day returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS 3-month trend and forward 20d returns stays below 0.03 across two consecutive rolling 2-year windows, or the sign of the correlation flips in more than one of three consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary easing cycle with FEDFUNDS near zero, rising HOUST signals rate-sensitive housing demand responding to cheap financing, which amplifies broader risk-asset appetite and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days; this link weakens or disappears when FEDFUNDS is elevated.
failure_condition: The HOUST-return relationship shows equal or greater predictive strength (Rank IC) during periods when FEDFUNDS > 2% compared to near-zero periods, indicating no regime dependency.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in INDPRO below its trailing 6-month average signals an abrupt supply-side contraction that markets have not yet fully priced, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls more than 1.5% below its trailing 6-month average
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the average triggered return is not significantly different from the unconditional average (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation accelerates past a threshold that raises expectations of future Fed tightening, S&P 500 forward 20-day returns turn negative as investors discount future rate hikes into current equity valuations.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns following CPI YoY > 3% are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05), or positive in more than 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime where UNRATE remains elevated but PAYEMS is rebounding, the joint signal of falling unemployment alongside rising payrolls captures the labor market inflection point, producing strong positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days that neither series alone fully captures.
failure_condition: The combined UNRATE-PAYEMS interaction signal fails to outperform the best single-series model by at least 0.02 Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window, indicating the interaction adds no incremental information.
